name: Quintanaluengos from Salinas de Pisuerga on the banks of the river Pisuerga
name_original: Quintanaluengos desde Salinas de Pisuerga a orillas del río Pisuerga
region_intro: Jogging around Salinas De Pisuerga offers a variety of running routes through its diverse landscape. The region is characterized by river valleys, such as the Pisuerga, and gentle hills, providing a mix of flat paths and moderate inclines. Runners can expect trails that traverse open countryside and offer views of the surrounding natural features. Best jogging routes around Salinas De Pisuerga The most popular jogging route is Quintanaluengos from Salinas de Pisuerga on the banks of the river Pisuerga, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 13 minutes to complete. This route follows the scenic banks of the Pisuerga river. Another top favourite among local runners is View of Santullán Valley loop from Brañosera, a moderate 3.6 miles (5.9 km) path. This loop offers views across the Santullán Valley with some elevation gain. Local runners also love the Casa de la Granja loop from Aguilar de Campoo, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 41 minutes. Jogging around Salinas De Pisuerga is defined by riverbanks, open valleys, and gentle hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Salinas De Pisuerga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Salinas De Pisuerga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes in the Salinas De Pisuerga area, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging trails through diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate, there are options suitable for beginners. The region's river valleys and gentle hills provide paths with minimal elevation gain. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120348763/quintanaluengos-from-salinas-de-pisuerga-on-the-banks-of-the-river-pisuerga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintanaluengos from Salinas de Pisuerga on the banks of the river Pisuerga&lt;/a&gt; route offers a relatively flat experience along the river.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on running trails in this region?
A: Running routes around Salinas De Pisuerga feature a varied landscape. You'll encounter paths along riverbanks, through open countryside, and over gentle hills. Elevation gains are generally moderate, though some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Solana – Mirador de la Umbría loop from Perapertú&lt;/a&gt;, offer more significant climbs for a challenging workout.

Q: Are there circular jogging routes available in Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: Yes, many of the running paths in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Santullán Valley loop from Brañosera&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic views and a satisfying loop experience.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see while running?
A: The region offers several scenic spots. You can enjoy views of the Santullán Valley, or explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Other natural highlights include the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roblón de Estalaya&lt;/a&gt;, a monumental oak tree, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5649482" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Valdecebollas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks accessible from the running routes?
A: Yes, you can discover historical elements while exploring. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5648200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corvio Necropolis&lt;/a&gt; is a notable historical site in the vicinity. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2065701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguilar de Campoo Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a significant man-made landmark near some routes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: The running routes in Salinas De Pisuerga are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from riverbanks to gentle hills, and the well-maintained paths that offer options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for experienced runners?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31457974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mesa loop from Aguilar de Campoo&lt;/a&gt; cover over 12 kilometers with moderate elevation, providing a good endurance test. The region has several difficult routes for experienced runners.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: The diverse landscape of Salinas De Pisuerga makes it suitable for jogging across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings and evenings are also ideal to avoid the midday heat, while winter can provide crisp, quiet runs, though conditions may vary.

Q: Are there family-friendly running options in the area?
A: Many of the easier and moderate routes along riverbanks or through flatter countryside are suitable for families looking to jog together. These paths often have less challenging terrain and provide a pleasant outdoor experience for all ages.

Q: Where can I find parking or access points for these running routes?
A: Access points and parking vary by route. Many trails start near towns like Salinas de Pisuerga or Aguilar de Campoo, where public parking is generally available. For specific routes, checking the komoot tour details will provide precise starting locations and potential parking information.

Q: Are there any quiet or less crowded running trails in Salinas De Pisuerga?
A: While popular routes can see more activity, the extensive network of trails means you can often find quieter paths, especially away from the main towns. Exploring some of the less-trafficked loops or out-and-back sections can offer a more serene running experience amidst nature.
tips:
Locality of the Palencia Mountain whose name comes, as they say, because it is located in a salty soil.
It has a parish church (San Pelayo) from the 16th century with a large bell tower.
It is worth visiting the medieval bridge that crosses the waters of the Pisuerga river, built in the 16th century.
